threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 48 bus is a reliable option. Locals know it’s hit or miss at best, especially on weekends.
Caught a sight of a bloke arguing with a lamppost at 3 AM on the bus to Streatham. Must’ve lost a bet or something; the lamppost didn’t budge.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy casually carrying a whole goat down the street for a BBQ in Tempelhof. That’s Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can find open-air karaoke in Görlitzer Park every summer. Just random strangers belting out 80s hits on a Wednesday night. Pure Berlin vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a true local if they instinctively avoid the Civic Center BART station after hearing one loud bang—better to walk than risk the chaos.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Spotted a Muni driver jump out to chase a runaway dog down Larkin. Took a solid five minutes but the pup made it back safe. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the sangria and tapas. Hardly anyone drinks that here; it's vermut and pa amb tomàquet if you want the real deal.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they hesitate at crosswalks, waiting for the green man, even when there’s no traffic.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's a 'quick walk' to the bus stop when it's really a 20-minute trek in heels. Good luck with that!
Caught the N42 last night and heard two lads arguing about whether you can buy a proper meat pie at 3 AM. Absolute madness.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a street artist painting a mural under the U-Bahn, while a kid sold homemade lemonade next to a guy juggling fire. That’s Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Last week, I saw a guy in a bathrobe at the U-Bahn station. Only in Kreuzberg would that be a normal morning commute.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a real local by their instinct to avoid BART delays; they’ll always suggest a bus route like it’s a secret shortcut nobody knows.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy trying to fix the Muni bus stop sign with duct tape. Smiled when he got a round of applause as the bus finally arrived. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the hip bars, but the real charm is in the quiet corners where old folks still gossip over café con leche.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their struggle with the metro map, fumbling through their phones while locals glide past effortlessly.
